summary.intent: Wire the existing CRM subsystem into the gateway's channel event pipeline so that inbound messages automatically create/update contacts and log interactions, completing CHA-15.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented CRM-gateway integration in ~4 files: added `handle_crm_inbound()` to `channel_events.rs`, hoisted CRM store in `server.rs`, added 8 passing unit tests, ran format/check, and committed the changes on branch `CHA-15-crm-gateway-integration`.

repo: CRM primitives (CrmStore trait, SqliteCrmStore, MemoryCrmStore, Contact/ContactChannel/Interaction types) were fully built but completely disconnected from the channel event pipeline — `channel_events.rs` had zero CRM references.
The project uses a `#[cfg(feature = "crm")]` feature flag throughout the gateway crate, with `crm` enabled by default.
CRM store access pattern follows the RetentionEngine pattern: pass `Arc<dyn CrmStore>` directly at construction, not via GatewayState.
The `GatewayChannelEventSink` uses a `tokio::sync::OnceCell<Arc<GatewayState>>` for deferred state access.

The `crm_service.rs` service layer uses `serde_json::Value` for all params/responses (the service trait mandates it), making it unsuitable for internal CRM operations — bypass it and use `CrmStore` trait directly.
Contact ID generation uses caller-provided UUIDs (`uuid::Uuid::new_v4()`) — the CRM crate does not auto-generate IDs.
The nightly rustfmt toolchain is `+nightly-2025-11-30` for this project.
`auto_create_contacts` config field exists in `CrmConfig` but was never read anywhere before this change.

The `crm_store` variable in `server.rs` was scoped inside a `#[cfg(feature = "crm")] { if config.crm.enabled { ... } }` block at line 1882, requiring hoisting to make it available at sink construction at line 2769.

code.finding: GatewayChannelEventSink struct — original only had `state: Arc<tokio::sync::OnceCell<Arc<GatewayState>>>`. Added `#[cfg(feature = "crm")] crm_store: Option<Arc<dyn moltis_crm::store::CrmStore>>` and `crm_config: moltis_config::schema::CrmConfig`.
emit() method serializes event to JSON, optionally renders QR SVG for WhatsApp, broadcasts to WebSocket — CRM hook added here for InboundMessage variant using tokio::spawn fire-and-forget.
crm_store is created inside a scoped cfg/if block — must hoist `crm_store_for_sink: Option<Arc<dyn CrmStore>>` above the block so it can be passed to the event sink constructor at line 2769.
Event sink constructed here — updated to call `.with_crm(crm_store_for_sink, crm_config)` builder method.
RetentionEngine creates a SEPARATE Arc<dyn CrmStore> from the same pool — this is the established pattern for giving subsystems direct store access without going through GatewayState.

workflow: The Stokowski/gstack orchestration pipeline runs CEO review → eng review → implement stages sequentially, each as a separate headless agent session.
Linear GraphQL `identifier` filter is not supported — must use `issues(filter: { team: { key: { eq: "CHA" } }, number: { eq: 15 } }, first: 1)` instead.
The context-mode hook blocks curl/wget — use Python3's `urllib.request` for HTTP calls in this environment.
ctx_batch_execute will timeout if slow commands like `cargo check` are included — run cargo commands separately from short grep/head commands.
Linear's `issueSearch` endpoint is deprecated; use `issues(filter: ...)` instead.
The eng review SMALL CHANGE mode provides a concrete implementation checklist (files, methods, test diagram) that makes the implement stage straightforward.
CEO review open questions should be resolved before the eng review stage so the implement stage has zero unresolved decisions.

friction: ctx_batch_execute timeout caused by slow `cargo check` — other commands in the batch were skipped, requiring separate re-runs.
Linear GraphQL `identifier` filter not supported — caused a validation error, had to switch to team key + number filter.
Linear `issueSearch` endpoint deprecated — returned deprecation error, required switching to `issues(filter: ...)`.
Linear API key not available in ctx_execute sandbox — env vars not inherited, had to fall back to Python3 via Bash.
curl/wget blocked by context-mode hook — required using Python3 urllib for HTTP calls.
crm_store scoping issue in server.rs — the store was created inside a nested cfg+if block, requiring variable hoisting to make it available at the sink construction site ~900 lines later.
channel_events.rs was large enough that multiple Read calls with different offsets were needed to see all relevant sections.
cargo check is slow on this workspace — adds significant latency to the quality gate loop.
Context window exhausted mid-session, requiring a context compaction and summary handoff before implementation was complete.
open_items: Follow-up ticket: Contact merge/dedup — same person on multiple channels creates separate contacts.
Follow-up ticket: Outbound interaction logging — only inbound messages create interactions currently.
Follow-up ticket: CRM Web UI — contact timeline in the web dashboard (RPC API only for now).
Follow-up ticket: GDPR documentation — document auto-contact creation privacy implications.
PR not yet created — `git push` and `gh pr create` were pending at end of transcript.
Linear workpad not yet updated with implementation completion summary.
Follow-up Linear tickets not yet created (contact merge/dedup, outbound logging, Web UI, GDPR docs).
Interaction dedup deliberately deferred — webhook retries can create duplicate CRM interactions.
Contact merge across channels deferred — same person on Telegram + WhatsApp = two separate contacts.
Matter auto-creation from messages not implemented — manual only.
Metrics (`crm.contacts.auto_created`, `crm.interactions.logged`, `crm.errors` counters) were specified in observability plan but implementation status not confirmed in transcript.
